picks.title: Holland Festival
picks.blurb: Netherlands' premier international theatre and dance festival with cutting-edge performances across multiple disciplines.

picks.title: John Summit
picks.blurb: Dance powerhouse plays exclusive European headline show blending house, techno and beyond.

picks.title: Bloodywood
picks.blurb: Indian metal/hip-hop fusion band brings high-energy performances to iconic venue.

picks.title: Queer Power
picks.blurb: Eye celebrates World Pride with films, talks and events showcasing queer makers and stories from around the world.
picks.why: Launches during World Pride Amsterdam weekend

picks.title: Danh Vo: πνεῦμα (Ἔλισσα)
picks.blurb: Vietnamese-Italian artist's philosophical meditation on breath, memory, and form—final weeks before closure.
picks.why: Closes Aug 2; weekend intro talks available

picks.title: Beyond the Manosphere: Masculinities Today
picks.blurb: Contemporary art survey examining toxic masculinity, identity, and gender through video, sculpture, and photography.

picks.title: Ed van der Elsken Photography
picks.blurb: Dutch photojournalist's 20th-century portraits opens this weekend at one of Europe's finest museums.

picks.title: The Little Big Things
picks.blurb: Theatre production opens Saturday; runs through August at Amsterdam's iconic concert hall venue.

picks.title: Opera in the Park: Simon Boccanegra
picks.blurb: Free screening of Verdi's Simon Boccanegra on giant outdoor screen. Bring picnic blanket and food for summer evening.

picks.title: Guns N' Roses
picks.blurb: Legendary rock band plays Ziggo Dome arena. One of this weekend's biggest live music events.

picks.title: Jardin Rouge Festival
picks.blurb: Multi-day music festival wrapping up this Saturday with diverse artists and eclectic programming.

picks.title: Guns N' Roses & Mammoth WVH
picks.blurb: Legendary rock band with special guest at Amsterdam's premier arena venue.

picks.title: Celeste
picks.blurb: British soul and R&B artist performing at Amsterdam's iconic mid-size venue.

picks.title: Jodeci
picks.blurb: 90s R&B legends bring their classic hits to one of Amsterdam's premier concert halls.

picks.title: Kho Liang Ie: Mid-Century Modernist
picks.blurb: Indonesian-Dutch pioneer's elegant furniture, interiors, and design—visual, tactile show ideal for families.

picks.title: GRIMM
picks.blurb: Hip-hop meets classical ballet in a wild reimagining of fairy tales—Cinderella, Snow White, and more get spectacular, darkly funny twists.

picks.title: Havenfestival 2026
picks.blurb: Waterfront celebration packed with free activities, demonstrations, and interactive experiences for families along the harbour.
picks.why: Free, outdoor, lots to explore for all ages

picks.title: Finale Koninklijk Concertgebouw Concours 2026
picks.blurb: Six short classical concerts showcasing young musicians competing in this prestigious final. Perfect introduction to live music for kids.
picks.why: Final weekend, accessible length for young listeners

picks.title: Royal Holland Cup – De Bosbaan
picks.blurb: International rowing races with 20+ countries competing, combined with festival atmosphere.

picks.title: Carel Visser Sculptures
picks.blurb: Thirteen large steel modernist sculptures by Dutch minimalist master in museum's outdoor garden.
picks.why: Free, opens mid-June, outdoor summer treat

picks.title: Solstice Art Festival
picks.blurb: Summer solstice celebration: open art market, live performances, artist installations, outdoor exhibits, free parking.

picks.title: Sunset Yoga
picks.blurb: Free summer solstice sunset yoga session organized by David Lloyd Clubs. End longest day with movement and nature.

picks.title: Bite of Amsterdam Food Festival
picks.blurb: Summer food festival featuring curated selection of local chefs, street food, and culinary experiences across Amsterdam's venues.

picks.title: Sunday Clay Club
picks.blurb: Beginner-friendly pottery workshop: explore hand-building and wheel techniques in relaxed, informal setting.

picks.title: Zeehavendagen Amsterdam – Seaport Days Festival
picks.blurb: Free harbour festival with guided ship tours, boat rides, demos, and family activities exploring Amsterdam's port.

picks.title: Open Tuinen Dagen (Open Garden Days)
picks.blurb: Explore 30 stunning private canal gardens hidden behind Amsterdam's historic façades in unique weekend tour.

picks.title: Open Garden Days Amsterdam
picks.blurb: Peek behind iconic canal houses into 30+ normally-closed private gardens. Annual weekend-only access to hidden green spaces.

picks.title: Eye Classics
picks.blurb: Classic films screened on the big screen in 35mm whenever possible, bringing cinema history to the forefront.
picks.why: Immersive film experience; 35mm projection a rare treat

picks.title: World Press Photo Exhibition
picks.blurb: Iconic annual photojournalism exhibition showcasing the world's most compelling documentary photography.
picks.why: Accessible, renowned exhibition open all weekend

picks.title: Tropico Latin Flavors
picks.blurb: Colombian ceviches, empanadas, and Bandeja Paisa draw South Americans across the Netherlands. Live music and karaoke make evenings lively.
picks.why: Vibrant South American spot with live entertainment

picks.title: Belly Pepper
picks.blurb: Turkish-inspired brunch with egg-topped börek towers and sucuk burritos. Seasonal menu, vegan and gluten-free options available.
picks.why: Cosy neighbourhood brunch spot with creative Turkish remixes

picks.title: Restaurant Kenneh
picks.blurb: The Netherlands' first West African fine dining restaurant from Liberian-Dutch chef Samuka Kenneh offers a refreshing break from French and Italian fine dining.
picks.why: Groundbreaking West African cuisine in fine dining format

picks.title: LuminAir Amsterdam
picks.blurb: High-vibe rooftop sky bar with city views near Central Station. Father's Day special: complimentary whisky cocktail.

picks.title: Bite of Amsterdam 2026
picks.blurb: Major food festival featuring top chefs, tastings, and culinary demos across Amsterdam's neighborhoods.
picks.why: 7 reasons foodies are getting tickets this weekend
picks.source: Your Little Black Book · Bite of Amsterdam / I amsterdam · Bite of Amsterdam official · I amsterdam

picks.title: Bacchus Wine Festival
picks.blurb: 300+ wines from around world, food trucks, live DJs, music & tasting sessions in forest setting.
picks.why: Wine festival with food pairing; outdoor three-day weekend e
picks.source: I amsterdam / Amsterdamian · Bacchus Wijnfestival / Amsterdam Spotted

picks.title: Open Garden Days
picks.blurb: Thirty private canal-house gardens normally closed to public open this rare weekend—centuries-old trees, ornamental details.

picks.title: Spinoza — De Mokum Musical
picks.blurb: Open-air musical about philosopher Spinoza set in old Amsterdam, in the forest theatre.
picks.why: Cross-source (LBB + I amsterdam) · open-air · dry-night plan
picks.source: Your Little Black Book · I amsterdam

picks.title: Hortus Botanicus
picks.blurb: One of the world’s oldest botanical gardens — a butterfly greenhouse and three-climate glasshouse to wander.
picks.source: Your Little Black Book / Hortus official · De Hortus

picks.title: The Movies — arthouse cinema
picks.blurb: The Netherlands’ oldest cinema still running — Art Deco rooms, a dinner-and-a-film deal, arthouse bills.
picks.why: Indoor · dinner + a film · perfect when it’s wet

picks.title: East Beach — rooftop city beach
picks.blurb: A new 800m² rooftop city beach above Oosterpark — sand, beach chairs, drinks, open to all.

picks.title: De Pimpelmees
picks.blurb: Cosy neighbourhood wine bar — European wines from €6, Italian snacks, free chips. Opened early 2026.

picks.title: Museum Market
picks.blurb: Sunday artisan market with local crafts, arts, food stalls, live music. Monthly neighbourhood gathering on Museumplein.

picks.title: 24 Hours Nieuw-West
picks.blurb: Free neighbourhood festival with sports, performances, music, art, food. Cruyff Court football tournaments alongside cultural activities.

picks.title: Zaanse Schans windmills
picks.blurb: Eight working windmills, cheese and crafts on the Zaan — free grounds, ~17 min train from Centraal.
picks.why: Short train · outdoor · classic kid day

picks.title: Haarlem — the easy escape
picks.blurb: A calmer historic city 15 min away — Grote Markt, the St-Bavo organ, a Saturday market and café-lined square.

picks.title: Foodhallen
picks.blurb: An indoor food hall in a converted tram depot — 20-odd stalls, long tables and two bars under one roof.
picks.why: Indoor · grazing for a crowd · weather-proof

picks.title: Moeders — Dutch home cooking
picks.blurb: Walls papered with strangers’ mothers’ photos and a stamppot-and-stew menu built for a grey evening.
picks.why: Snug · classic Dutch · ideal in the rain

picks.title: Café de Klos — ribs & grill
picks.blurb: A tiny, packed grill house off the canals famous for sticky spare ribs — no frills, no reservations.
picks.why: Warm · cheap · duck in out of the wet

picks.title: Wynand Fockink — jenever tasting
picks.blurb: A 1679 tasting room behind Dam Square pouring its own jenevers and liqueurs — you bend to the brimful glass.
picks.why: Historic · indoor · made for a downpour

picks.title: Café Chris — oldest brown café
picks.blurb: Open since 1624: a creaky, golden-lit Jordaan brown café for a slow afternoon beer while it pours outside.

picks.title: Pllek — waterfront hang
picks.blurb: A cargo-container café-bar on the NDSM wharf — big windows onto the IJ, sofas, and a wood stove when it turns.
picks.why: Indoor seating · IJ views · cozy when grey

picks.title: Madurodam
picks.blurb: The whole of the Netherlands at 1:25 — miniature canals, Schiphol and windmills kids can tower over.

picks.title: Efteling
picks.blurb: Europe’s oldest fairytale theme park — enchanted forest, dark rides and proper coasters in a storybook world.

picks.title: The Frozen Fountain
picks.blurb: The country’s best-known design store — floors of furniture, lighting and covetable objects to browse.

picks.title: Brewery Homeland
picks.blurb: A craft brewery and beach bar on the IJ — tank-fresh beer and a waterfront sun-trap terrace.

picks.title: Vlieger
picks.blurb: A two-floor temple to paper since 1869 — hundreds of colours, handmade sheets and card stacked to the ceiling.

picks.title: Athenaeum Boekhandel
picks.blurb: The intellectual heart of the Spui — a beloved bookshop and an international newsstand packed floor-to-ceiling.

picks.title: The American Book Center
picks.blurb: A sprawling three-floor English-language bookshop — a spiral of shelves and a basement of zines and comics.

picks.title: Patta
picks.blurb: The homegrown streetwear label and culture hub — drops, sneakers and the community that put Amsterdam on the map.

picks.title: Moise
picks.blurb: An understated De Pijp boutique with a razor-sharp eye — Acne, Lemaire, Marant and the like, hand-picked and beautifully shown.

picks.title: Cowboys to Catwalk
picks.blurb: A treasure-trove of vintage designer — Margiela, Marc Jacobs and one-off finds rummaged from decades of fashion.

picks.title: Mobilia
picks.blurb: Three floors of high design on the Utrechtsestraat — icons by the masters alongside more attainable covetables.
